name: LLM Cost Optimization
description: Techniques to reduce inference cost while preserving output quality: semantic caching (reusing responses for similar queries), model routing (directing queries to the cheapest model that can handle them), speculative decoding, prompt compression, and quantization. At scale, inference costs dominate — a 10x cost reduction can change the economics of a product entirely.
contrarian_take: Semantic caching papers report hit rates on benchmark datasets where query distributions are stationary — not the adversarial, long-tail query distributions of real products. Model routing papers benchmark routing accuracy but rarely report total system cost including the router's own inference cost. Papers that report end-to-end cost under realistic production query distributions, not benchmark hit rates, are telling the truth.
example_signals: FrugalGPT: How to Use Large Language Models While Reducing Cost and Improving Performance
RouteLLM: Learning to Route LLMs with Preference Data
GPTCache: A Data or Prompt Caching System for LLM-Based Applications
